scenario_title: Replaying an idempotent delete_rr on absent data
test_rationale: This test isolates the underspecified replay semantics of delete_rr by targeting a TXT RR that is absent from the start while keeping the enclosing name present, making the UPDATE syntactically valid and prerequisite-safe. The only meaningful observation is the UPDATE reply code, since the follow-up query should confirm the same empty TXT RRset in all cases. That makes the scenario a clean reply-only divergence probe for client retry behavior.

group_1.behavior: Treats replayed delete_rr of absent data as an idempotent no-op and returns success.
group_1.post_state: No TXT RRset at retry.campus.edu.; the zone is unchanged.

group_2.behavior: Treats deletion of already-absent data as a semantic failure for the UPDATE.
group_2.post_state: No TXT RRset at retry.campus.edu.; the zone is unchanged.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2.8 defines delete_rr as removing a specific RR, but it does not state what status to return when the RR is already absent. The atomic UPDATE model also does not force a unique outcome for a replayed no-op delete, so implementations may diverge on whether they report success or NXRRSET-like failure.
